title: papaver bhutanicum
intro: Papaver bhutanicum, also known as the Bhutan Poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family, characterized by its vibrant red and white flowers and delicate, papery leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid compounds, including thebaine and codeine. The medicinal properties of Papaver bhutanicum have been found to have various health benefits, including pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved sleep quality. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or tincture for medicinal use, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
